title: Dog Of Death

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic catchphrase itself
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - culturally iconic, perfectly economical, and uniquely his character

jokes.text: I don't need your crummy job. I won the lottery! Well, I won the lottery too! We both won the lottery! Why don't you win the lottery too?
jokes.setup: Fantasy of quitting job after winning lottery
jokes.punchline: Everyone claiming to have won, then inviting others to join the delusion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing collective delusion about lottery winning

jokes.text: Where everybody wins! Actual odds of winning: one in 380 million. The lottery-- exploiter of the poor and ignorant.
jokes.setup: Cheerful lottery slogan about everyone winning
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction with terrible odds and harsh reality
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical contrast between marketing and reality

jokes.text: Here's your lottery ticket. Thank you for knocking over my inventory. Come again.
jokes.setup: Customer causing damage in store
jokes.punchline: Apu's polite, resigned acceptance while still being courteous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu character moment - polite even when being abused

jokes.text: What's Lisa's birthday? What? You don't know your sister's birthday?
jokes.setup: Homer asking Bart for lucky numbers
jokes.punchline: Homer doesn't know his own daughter's birthday
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer neglectful parenting moment

jokes.text: Strap on your bulletproof vest. It's time for another bank run. If I don't make it... promise you won't sleep with my wife. I promise nothing.
jokes.setup: Dramatic departure with noble request about wife
jokes.punchline: Blunt refusal to make that promise
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark honesty subverts melodramatic setup

jokes.text: The lottery is the one ray of hope... in my otherwise unbearable life! Uh, the lottery and you.
jokes.setup: Homer declaring lottery as only hope
jokes.punchline: Awkward afterthought remembering to include his wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - selfish then trying to recover with terrible timing

jokes.text: Yes, money-- I mean honey.
jokes.setup: Homer promising not to get carried away
jokes.punchline: Freudian slip revealing his true priorities
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Freudian slip joke

jokes.text: In fact, every copy of Shirley Jackson's The Lottery has been checked out from the library. The book does not contain hints on winning the lottery. It is a chilling tale of conformity gone mad.
jokes.setup: People checking out book titled 'The Lottery'
jokes.punchline: It's actually a dark story about ritualistic murder, not gambling tips
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference joke about public's lottery obsession

jokes.text: Just think what we can buy with that money-- history books that know how the Korean War came out... and a state-of-the-art detention hall... where children are held in place with magnets.
jokes.setup: School official excited about lottery money for education
jokes.punchline: Outdated textbooks and bizarre punishment methods with magnets
jokes.explanation: Good satire of underfunded schools with absurd magnetic detention

jokes.text: Magnets-- always with the magnets.
jokes.setup: School's obsession with magnetic detention
jokes.punchline: Exasperated observation about overuse of magnets
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to the magnet joke, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Homer, the odds are 380 million to one. Correction: 380 million to 50!
jokes.setup: Marge stating the astronomical odds
jokes.punchline: Homer's delusional math thinking buying 50 tickets makes him a favorite
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely wrong math delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Bank it. Give it to the poor. What do kids know about spending money?
jokes.setup: Kids giving responsible answers about lottery winnings
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissing their wisdom despite them being more mature than him
jokes.explanation: Good irony - kids more responsible than their father

jokes.text: Look closer, Lenny. Oh, you're the biggest man in the world now... and you're covered in gold. 14-karat gold.
jokes.setup: Homer fantasizing about lottery wealth
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous vision of being literally covered in gold
jokes.explanation: Absurd wealth fantasy, though fairly predictable

jokes.text: I've got so many tickets I can't lose. The first number is 17. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's overconfidence about his many tickets
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation with first number
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/punchline with Homer's signature catchphrase

jokes.text: If you were 17, we'd be rich. But no, you had to be ten.
jokes.setup: Family losing lottery
jokes.punchline: Homer blaming Bart's age for the loss
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic - blaming child for being the wrong age

jokes.text: The winner of today's state lottery is... me, Kent Brockman. Can we get a shot of me? There you go. In other news, uh... a tragic mix-up today in Cleveland. Many people killed. Uh... good-bye!
jokes.setup: News anchor reporting lottery winner
jokes.punchline: Unprofessional self-interest followed by dismissive treatment of tragedy
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing news anchor's ego and callousness

jokes.text: Oh, well, we lost the money... but we've still got each other. Hey, the dog's dead.
jokes.setup: Marge's touching sentiment about family togetherness
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt announcement immediately deflating the moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic structure - touching moment instantly destroyed by terrible timing

jokes.text: He's not dead. Don't say the dog's dead when he's not. It's not fair to toy with people's emotions. He is dead. I'll get a shovel.
jokes.setup: Marge defending the dog's life
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact preparation for burial
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the death debate with Homer's practical response

jokes.text: If Grandpa says he's dead, he must be alive.
jokes.setup: Grandpa's assessment of the dog's condition
jokes.punchline: Homer's reverse logic based on Grandpa's unreliability
jokes.explanation: Clever reverse psychology joke about Grandpa's senility

jokes.text: Dogs wag their tails for hours after they die.
jokes.setup: Dog wagging tail as sign of life
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's morbid 'fact' about post-mortem tail wagging
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny fake fact that sounds plausible from senile Grandpa

jokes.text: I love animals. I spend my life saving them, and they can't thank me. The parrots can, but--
jokes.setup: Veterinarian lamenting animals can't thank him
jokes.punchline: Exception for parrots who can speak
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor with logical exception

jokes.text: In doggie heaven, there's mountains of bones... and you can't turn around... without sniffing another dog's butt.
jokes.setup: Homer describing doggie heaven to comfort kids
jokes.punchline: Including crude but dog-appropriate activities
jokes.explanation: Crude but logical extension of dog behavior to afterlife

jokes.text: One of the Lassies is in there too. The mean one-- the one that mauled Timmy.
jokes.setup: Discussion of doggie hell inhabitants
jokes.punchline: Subverting Lassie's heroic image with violence
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of beloved TV dog's wholesome image

jokes.text: Lousy, dog-killing son-of-a-- Well, it will be okay. We'll get him a new dog-- one with an untwistable stomach.
jokes.setup: Bart's anger about letting the dog die
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd solution - a dog with better stomach engineering
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical absurd problem-solving approach

jokes.text: Dogs are idiots. If I came into your house... and started sniffing at your crotch... and slobbering all over your face... what would you say? If you did it, sir? Exactly. You'd be fit to be tied.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining why he hates dogs
jokes.punchline: Smithers' implication that he'd be fine if Burns did those things
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns/Smithers dynamic with unexpected sexual subtext

jokes.text: You are not performing that operation yourself. But, Marge, it looks so easy. Just like carving a turkey.
jokes.setup: Homer considering DIY surgery
jokes.punchline: Comparing delicate veterinary surgery to carving meat
jokes.explanation: Horrifying Homer logic comparing surgery to food preparation

jokes.text: Fried chicken night will be organ meat night. Ham night will be spam night, and pork chop night... will be chub night. I don't even know what that is.
jokes.setup: Marge listing food budget cuts
jokes.punchline: Descending quality ending with mysterious 'chub'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of increasingly disgusting food substitutions

jokes.text: But next week is volume four-- Copernicus through elephantiasis.
jokes.setup: Lisa upset about losing her encyclopedia subscription
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and unfortunate alphabetical range
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment showing her intellectual interests, with the humor coming from the unfortunate disease being grouped with astronomy

jokes.text: Marge, I've figured out... an alternative to giving up my beer. Basically, we become a family of traveling acrobats. I don't think you've thought this through.
jokes.setup: Homer finding alternative to sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Completely impractical circus family solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer avoidance logic - choosing impossible over simple sacrifice

jokes.text: We saved your gamecock... but he'll never fight again. That's what you think. He'll fight and he'll win.
jokes.setup: Doctor delivering bad news about fighting rooster
jokes.punchline: Owner's delusional determination despite medical advice
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about animal cruelty and stubborn denial

jokes.text: It's times like this... I'm glad I flunked out of dental school.
jokes.setup: Successful animal surgery
jokes.punchline: Veterinarian's relief about not working on humans
jokes.explanation: Mild professional humor about career path

jokes.text: Lousy chub night. Hey, how come... he gets meat and we don't? You wouldn't want what he's eating. It's mostly snouts and entrails. Mmm... snouts.
jokes.setup: Family on food budget eating chub while dog gets meat
jokes.punchline: Homer's appetite even for disgusting dog food ingredients
jokes.explanation: Good callback to chub joke with Homer's typical food obsession

jokes.text: He's got all the money in the world... but there's one thing he can't buy. What's that? A dinosaur.
jokes.setup: Classic 'money can't buy everything' setup
jokes.punchline: Unexpected and absurd answer - dinosaurs
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of expected moral lesson with absurd child logic

jokes.text: Here, lottery officials present Springfield Elementary... with a brand-new eraser. One eraser? I'm used to my government betraying me. I served for--
jokes.setup: School getting lottery money for supplies
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small contribution - just one eraser
jokes.explanation: Good satire of government funding inadequacy

jokes.text: Oh, no! Those are my numbers. If it wasn't for that dog, I would have won.
jokes.setup: Homer hearing winning lottery numbers after spending money on dog surgery
jokes.punchline: Blaming the dog for his lottery loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer selfishness - blaming dog he saved for his bad luck

jokes.text: Who's that old rummy? Before his dog got sick... that old rummy used to be my best customer.
jokes.setup: Homer begging for quarters outside bar
jokes.punchline: Moe identifying his former best customer now reduced to busking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's decline from regular customer

jokes.text: I know you can't understand me... but you're a lousy dog and I hate you.
jokes.setup: Homer alone with Santa's Little Helper
jokes.punchline: Cruel honesty to dog he thinks can't understand
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer moment showing his resentment toward the dog

jokes.text: I know you can't understand me... but you're a lousy dog and I hate you.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing the dog directly
jokes.punchline: Cruel honesty delivered to an animal that can't understand
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest character moment showing Homer's resentment and self-awareness

jokes.text: Oh, you mean tonight. The dog will get out.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if Homer closed the gate
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed realization he meant a different time
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer confusion moment

jokes.text: Wait! There he is! No, that's a horse.
jokes.setup: Searching for lost dog
jokes.punchline: Mistaking horse for dog
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag, not particularly clever

jokes.text: I can't wait to start pawing through my garbage... like some starving raccoon.
jokes.setup: Environmental presentation about recycling
jokes.punchline: Burns' sarcastic dismissal comparing it to animal behavior
jokes.explanation: Good Burns character moment - dismissive of environmental efforts

jokes.text: He's been here since the late '60s. I'll never forget the day he bagged his first hippie. That young man didn't think it was too groovy.
jokes.setup: Discussing old attack dog's history
jokes.punchline: Violent attack on hippie with period-appropriate slang
jokes.explanation: Dark humor combining violence with hippie-era nostalgia

jokes.text: Wiry, fast, firm, proud buttocks. Reminds me of me.
jokes.setup: Burns evaluating attack dog qualities
jokes.punchline: Comparing dog's buttocks to his own
jokes.explanation: Typical Burns vanity moment with inappropriate comparison

jokes.text: If that was a Girl Scout... I'd have been bothered by now.
jokes.setup: Testing attack dog on child selling cookies
jokes.punchline: Implying the dog should have attacked an innocent child
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about training dogs to attack children

jokes.text: Remember? Doggie heaven? Oh, Marge! There is no such place. Or... to put it another way... there is.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to comfort Homer about dog's death
jokes.punchline: Homer crushing then awkwardly trying to restore hope
jokes.explanation: Homer's clumsy attempt to undo his own cruelty

jokes.text: Since I won the lottery... everybody wants a piece of Kent Brockman-- homeless this and hungry that.
jokes.setup: Kent complaining about people asking for money
jokes.punchline: Dismissive attitude toward legitimate human suffering
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kent's newfound callousness

jokes.text: Sir, your llama just bit Ted Kennedy. Good!
jokes.setup: Reporting llama attack on politician
jokes.punchline: Kent's approval of political figure being bitten
jokes.explanation: Absurd wealthy behavior with political bite

jokes.text: You can pet the cat. The cat? What's the point?
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to pet the dog again
jokes.punchline: Dismissive attitude toward cats as inferior pets
jokes.explanation: Simple pet preference joke

tmdb_overview: Santa's Little Helper notices resentment after the Simpsons make sacrifices to fund his life-saving operation.

guest_stars.name: Frank Welker
guest_stars.character: Santa's Little Helper / Mr. Teeny / Hounds (voice)

insight: Santa's Little Helper's illness anchors 49 jokes at moderate density, balancing heartfelt stakes with absurdist comedy.
